title: [lichen_util.h:53] secure_zero silent NULL return in release
description: Function: secure_zero. Contract violation: The function silently returns on NULL pointer in release builds (line 58: if (ptr == NULL || len == 0) return) but only asserts in debug builds. This inconsistent behavior means callers won't notice a programming error in production. A misused secure_zero could leave sensitive data unzeroed. Consider always returning an error code or using a compiler warning attribute. Priority 2 because this is a security-sensitive function where silent failures are problematic.
close_reason: Fixed: Added explicit NULL check with printk warning before early return. The function now logs 'WARN: secure_zero called with NULL pointer' in release builds instead of silently returning. Also added #include <zephyr/sys/printk.h>.
